title: Rotary Station
nearby_text: 3.2.1: Rotary Station Â©2025 Fortna Inc. 9 3.2.1: ROTARY STATION CAUTION PINCH POINT Be careful to not pinch fingers between the chute and the station when rotating the bag racks. Failure to follow these instructions can result in injury or equipment damage. The rotary station, located under the chute, has bag racks for parcel bags and can be rotated so the oper- ator can access additional bags. The bags are manually replenished by operators. There is one rotary station per operator station. While bags are being filled, the foot brake in the middle of the station needs to be engaged. Before rotating, make sure the foot brake is disengaged. FOOT BRAKE Figure 3-2: Rotary Station

nearby_text: 4.1.1: System HMI Screens 24 Â©2025 Fortna Inc. 4.1.1.5.2: AGV API CONTROLS The AGVs can be controlled in the AGVs section of the API screen. Figure 4-12: AGV API Controls Controls Description Remove AGV An AGV can be removed from the system by entering the AGV ID. If and AGV has an issue, it must be physically removed from the system. If the AGV has a tote, it must be taken to the hospital where the tote is removed. The tote is then "recovered" when it is put back in the system. Recover AGV Enter an AGV ID to add it to the system. Pause All AGVs Pauses all AGV movement. Resume All AGVs Resumes all AGV movement. Execute AGV Command This is used for a system recover in the event that the system goes down and messages are missed. Resend Geek Com- mand This is also used for a system recover. If Geek+ has a command error, the command guide can be provided and the message will be resent to Geek+. title: Operator Station HMI Data Screenâ€”Heartbeat Stats

nearby_text: 4.1.2: Operator Station HMI Screens 36 Â©2025 Fortna Inc. Figure 4-22: Operator Station HMI Data Screenâ€”Heartbeat Stats Description The "Heartbeat" section provides statistics for heartbeat signals. l Last: How long, in milliseconds, the most recent signal between the tipper and WCS took. l Max: How long, in milliseconds, the longest signal between the tipper and WCS took. A heartbeat signal longer than 10 seconds can cause operation issues with the tipper due to mis-synchronization. l Min: How long, in milliseconds, the shortest signal between the tipper and WCS took. l RESET button: Press this button to reset the data to zero. title: System API Controls
nearby_text: 4.1.1: System HMI Screens 22 Â©2025 Fortna Inc. 4.1.1.5.1: SYSTEM API CONTROLS The state of the system can be found in the System section of the API screen. The system can also be started, shut down, and purged using this section of the screen. Figure 4-9: System API Controls Control Description Get System State Displays the current state of OptiSweep. System Startup Allows the user to put the system in a running state. System Shutdown Shuts down the system. The AGVs go to a shutdown queue. This should only be done when a sort does not begin when the current sort ends. System Close Out All totes are sent to the tippers to close out the sorter. Close Out should only be done at the end of a sort. System Purge All totes are sent to the tippers. Labels are not printed during a purge. Reset Metrics Reset the metrics. This should only be done between sorts. Table 4-1: System API Controls Description Clicking GET METRICS pops up a screen showing several metrics, including Number of Exchanges, Number of Mis-sorts, and Status.

title: System in Shut-down Mode
nearby_text: 5.8: AGV Faults 72 Â©2025 Fortna Inc. 5.8: AGV FAULTS The following section provide instructions for the startup and shut down of the AGVs, and guidance on recovering AGVs from their faulted states to get them back in service. 5.8.1: AGV SYSTEM START-UP Before starting the system, make sure there are enough AGVs without totes in the system. There should be nine (9) AGVs without totes in the system: five (5) under the north rack and four (4) under the south rack. Figure 5-1: System in Shut-down Mode If the system is started without nine totes, an AGV without a tote will need to be moved into the zone that is missing an AGV. Make sure to add the AGV to the RMS after moving the AGV. NOTE: Make sure to start the system 15 minutes before the sort starts. To start the system: 1. Reset the metrics. This makes pulling reports easier. 2. Start the system. 3. Close out. After close out (Bag Out) is completed, the system goes back to "Running". 4. Shut down.

Nearby text states that before starting the system there should be nine AGVs without totes in the system, with five under the north rack and four under the south rack. It also notes that if the system is started without nine totes, an AGV without a tote must be moved into the missing zone and added to the RMS. The startup sequence described nearby includes resetting metrics, starting the system, completing close out or Bag Out, and returning the system to Running. title: Add AGV to Zone with No Empty AGV Screen
caption_text: Figure 5-3: Add AGV to Zone with No Empty AGV Screen
nearby_text: 5.8.2: Fault Recovery 74 Â©2025 Fortna Inc. 5.8.2.2: ADD AGV TO ZONE WITH NO EMPTY AGV Figure 5-3: Add AGV to Zone with No Empty AGV Screen 1. E-stop RMS. 2. Move an AGV into the zone under the tote rack. 3. Add AGV in RMS. (See "Tote API Controls" on page 26.) 4. Wait for AGV to turn blue. 5. Verify AGV status is "szonestaging". (Reference AGV state on HMI.). If the AGV is moving to the Hospital, remove the AGV and add it again. 6. Press E-stop again. 7. Resume RMS. (See "Starting the System" on page 65.) 5.8.2.3: AGVS LOST TASKS If empty AGVs have lost their tasks because the RMS was restarted: 1. E-stop RMS. 2. Remove and re-add AGVs without totes twice. (See "Tote API Controls" on page 26.) 3. If the system needs to be restarted, make sure the AGVs are in the "szonestaging" state. (Reference AGV state on the HMI.) 4. Press the E-stop again when ready to resume. 5. Resume RMS. (See "Starting the System" on page 65.) NOTE: The best practice is to shut down and restart to ensure everything is in sync.

The nearby text also notes that if the AGV is moving to the Hospital, the operator should remove the AGV and add it again, then press E-stop again and resume RMS. title: Cable Carrier
nearby_text: 7.3.16: Z-Axis Cable Carrier Replacement Â©2025 Fortna Inc. 153 7.3.16: Z-AXIS CABLE CARRIER REPLACEMENT The Z-Axis cable carrier can be fully replaced or repaired if a limited number of links are broken. The cable carrier snaps together and can be snapped apart by hand or with the help of a small standard screwdriver. The Z-Axis cable carrier includes dividers between the cable and the air line in every second link. It is acceptable for several of these dividers to be missing. The dividers should be all the way to one side against the air line to prevent the air line from contacting the FFC. Required Tools l Replacement Part: Z-Axis Cable Carrier l PPE: Safety glasses, gloves l Tools Needed: Standard screwdriver, 1.5-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench/socket, l Time Required: 1 hour CABLE CARRIER Figure 7-17: Cable Carrier

These screws will prevent the A-Axis and counterbalance from dropping once belt tension is released.

nearby_text: 5.2: Stopping The System 66 Â©2025 Fortna Inc. 5.2: STOPPING THE SYSTEM NOTE: The system should be shutdown between sorts to allow AGVs to charge. 1. On the system HMI, navigate to the system API screen: 2. Press SYSTEM SHUTDOWN. 3. The AGVs go to a shutdown queue. 5.3: STARTING THE OPERATOR STATION If the system has been turned off, follow the procedure below to start the system: 1. Verify the AGVs are powered on. Reference P40A (V2.0) user manual, Document version: V1.1 (20231206) for instructions on starting the AGVs. 2. Verify the main disconnect is ON. 3. At the operator station control panel, if the ESTOP is flashing, pull out the ESTOP and press RESET on the HMI screen to clear any faults. 4. "Home" every axis: Z1 and A1 (left tipper) and Z2 and A2 (right tipper): a. Press AUTO REF. The axis button changes from green to yellow to indicate it is moving. The tipper motor moves until it senses the "home" proximity switch, then stops. 5. Press CYCLE START. The motor moves from "home" to "ready." 6. Place the tipper in AUTO mode. The tipper will wait for the WCS to send instructions. 5.4: STOPPING THE OPERATOR STATION 1. Press CYCLE STOP on the operator station main screen.

nearby_text: 5.10.1: Tote Initialization Procedure 78 Â©2025 Fortna Inc. 5.10.1: TOTE INITIALIZATION PROCEDURE To initialize totes at the sorter using the Zebra scanner, follow the procedure below: 1. Open the tote scanning application on the Hospital laptop and select "Sorter Destinations" from the main menu. 2. Approach the sorter and scan the tote sorter stand position barcode. This barcode is located in the center of the rack on the top rail. 3. Scan the tote barcode of the tote being placed in the stand. NOTE: The order of scanning the position and tote barcodes does not affect the process. 4. If the scan pairing is successful, the scanner will flash green. If the pairing is unsuccessful, the scanner will flash yellow, indicating that rescanning is required. 5. Once the new set of scans is successfully completed, no further action is required. The oper- ator can return the scanner application to Hospital Mode for any subsequent tasks.

All Z1 motors in every operator station will have the same parameters; and all Z2 motors in every operator station will have the same configuration, so if a new Z1 motor is installed, the parameters can be taken from any other Z1 motor and uploaded to the new motor. 7.3.5.2: MOTOR REMOVAL 1.

nearby_text: 7.3.24: Scanner Setup 194 Â©2025 Fortna Inc. 7.3.24: SCANNER SETUP If a scanner is replaced, the new scanner needs to be setup. Requirements: l Windows 10 or 11 laptop with "Barcode Interface.exe" installed (found on the desktop with a FORTNA logo). l Zebra DS3608-SR scanner dock, USB cable, power adapter l 120 VAC outlet (x2) l Network connectivity to the AGV and Hospital control at the laptop. Validate connectivity by pinging their IP addresses. Follow the procedure below to setup the new scanner: 1. Connect the RJ45 side of the USB cable to the back of the scanner dock, the USB to the laptop, and the auxiliary to the power adapter. Be sure to plug the adapter into a 120 VAC out- let. The dock should light up. NOTE: If the scanner is uncharged it will need to rest on the dock until the red battery symbol shown on top turns green. 2. Log into the laptop with a FORTNA account. 3. Launch the Barcode Interface application. This should be on the desktop. 4. Check connectivity on the bottom right of the screen. a. If green, skip this step. b. If red, click "Calibrate Scanner" and follow the onscreen instructions.
retrieval_text: Page 210, section 7.3.24 Scanner Setup, covers setup of a replacement scanner in the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual. The nearby text states that if a scanner is replaced, the new scanner must be set up using a Windows 10 or 11 laptop with Barcode Interface.exe, a Zebra DS3608-SR scanner dock, USB cable, power adapter, and two 120 VAC outlets. It also requires network connectivity to the AGV and Hospital control, validated by pinging their IP addresses. The procedure includes connecting the dock, confirming the dock lights up, waiting for the scanner battery indicator to change from red to green if uncharged, logging in with a FORTNA account, launching Barcode Interface, checking the bottom-right connectivity indicator, and using Calibrate Scanner if the indicator is red. This artifact is relevant for scanner replacement, barcode interface setup, dock connection, charging status, and connectivity troubleshooting.

retrieval_text: Page 212 of the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual, section 7.3.25 "After a Power Outage," references a System HMI screen used for outage recovery. The nearby instructions state that if a power outage occurs in the middle of a sort and lasts long enough for systems to lose communication, the operator should navigate to the "ACB API" screen and press "SYSTEM PURGE" in the System section. This causes AGVs with totes to go to operator stations so the totes can be emptied. The source also notes that labels will not be printed for bags and packages must be re-circulated to the sorter lane. short_description: AGV System HMI screen reference associated with the ACB API screen and SYSTEM PURGE action used after a power outage.
detailed_description: This artifact appears in the manual section "7.3.25: After a Power Outage" and is associated with instructions for recovering from a power outage that interrupts a sort long enough for systems to lose communication. The nearby text directs the user to navigate on the System HMI to the "ACB API" screen and, in the "System" section, press "SYSTEM PURGE." The purpose of this action is to send AGVs carrying totes to operator stations so the totes can be emptied. The text also notes an important operational consequence: labels will not be printed for bags, so packages must be re-circulated to the sorter lane. This artifact is useful as a visual anchor for locating the correct HMI screen during outage recovery, although the exact on-screen details are not confirmed from the extracted data.
